question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBatch().getQueueCapacity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sustained burst of valid Hedera transactions that generate record files faster than the parser consumes them, causing the ArrayBlockingQueue (sized by queueCapacity=10) to fill completely and block the BatchStreamFileNotifier thread indefinitely via queue.put(), halting all downstream stream file processing and causing a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBatch().getWindow()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the window=5m batching window by submitting a large volume of transactions that keep the importer perpetually behind, causing the batch accumulation logic to continuously hold large numbers of stream files in memory for up to 5 minutes, exhausting heap and causing a non-network-based DoS?
